tagline: Coordinating Vietnamese presence at Greater Sacramento civic parades

org.name: Community Partners Advocate of Little Saigon Sacramento

pitch.headline: Show up together. Meet new friends before you march.
pitch.body: Little Saigon Krewe coordinates Vietnamese presence at civic parades across Greater Sacramento. On Golden Harvest morning you’ll check in around 9:00, line up with peers who share your world — high school with high school, college with college, and so on — and march at 11:00 as a train of krewe groups, one after another. Named Tribe Champions and greeters help groups connect with peers in other groups, so everyone feels welcome in the broader community.

tribes.name: High school
tribes.peers: High school clubs — VSAs and culture clubs; interclub bonding with a light cross-chapter mix
tribes.championProfile: Someone vested in high school clubs (e.g. a campus VSA outreach lead)

tribes.name: College
tribes.peers: Campus clubs, e.g. VSAs and VN CARES chapters
tribes.championProfile: A campus club officer (VSA or VN CARES) who already convenes peers

tribes.name: Performance
tribes.peers: Lion, dance, and troupe warm-up · spacing · music cues
tribes.championProfile: An active dancer or lion lead who needs coordination anyway

tribes.name: Business
tribes.peers: Chambers and merchants — meet peers on foot in the marching block
tribes.championProfile: A merchant or chamber face who wants peers to meet each other

tribes.name: Family & heritage
tribes.peers: Language school, scouts, pageant, veterans, elders
tribes.championProfile: Someone already in the language / scout / pageant / elder orbit

tribes.name: Service
tribes.championProfile: A peer nonprofit lead (when two or more service orgs show)

tribes.name: Floats & vehicles
tribes.peers: Anyone bringing a float, trailer, car, or pickup — stages on the vehicle block, separate from the marching line-up.
tribes.championProfile: Someone bringing a vehicle entry, or comfortable running the vehicle line-up

championAsk.headline: Tribe Champions
championAsk.body: Each peer tribe aims for a pair of champions — stage managers, not stars — who hold the tribe sign and help people connect before the march. We’ll post confirmed names here as people step up. Until then, seats are open.

ctaCard.title: Want to be a tribe champion?
ctaCard.body: Pick a tribe that fits you — or tell us where you’d help most. We aim for a pair on each tribe.

wishlist.headline: Krewe wishlist
wishlist.body: In-kind needs for Golden Harvest — like a gift registry. No dollar amounts yet. Email to pledge what you can cover.

items.detail: Hospitality during the staging window (~9:00–11:00) before the march. Packaged meals, snacks, or gift vouchers all work.

items.need: Food, gas, and parking for a UC Davis club
items.detail: Help a UC Davis chapter get to Capitol Mall and stay fed — day-of meals or vouchers, gas for carpools, and downtown parking near staging.

transit.headline: Getting there
transit.intro: Staging is on 9th Street at Capitol Mall. Check-in is about 9:00. You can take SacRT (free rides by coupon in 2025 — expect similar for 2026) or drive and park nearby. If your group is bringing a float or parade vehicle, that uses the separate float line-up — not the public garages.
transit.stagingTitle: Where to walk (from 2025 packet — expect similar for 2026)
staging: 9th St between L St and N St · 9:00–10:45
9th St between S Capitol Mall and N St
9th St between L St and N Capitol Mall — Tribe G
transit.parkingLead: Carpools are encouraged. Street parking where available, or these lots near Capitol Mall (2025 rates — confirm in the 2026 packet):
parking: ~$2 / half hour, $7 daily max (6 AM–5 PM)
~$2 / half hour, $7 daily max (6 AM–5 PM)
steps: Take a SacRT Elk Grove local bus toward Cosumnes River College (CRC) — the southern end of the Blue Line.
Board Blue Line light rail toward Watt / I-80 (downtown direction).
Exit at 8th & Capitol Station. Walk one block east to 9th St — check-in and marching line-up are on 9th at Capitol Mall.
Return: board Blue Line at 8th & Capitol toward Cosumnes River College (check the headsign), then Elk Grove bus home.
notes: Free RT (2025): SacRT provided free rides by scanning a coupon — look for the 2026 coupon in the Golden Harvest packet or from parade organizers.
Saturday schedules differ from weekday rush-hour — Elk Grove weekday-only commuter buses (e.g. E13) won’t help parade morning. Use local EG routes + Blue Line, and verify times the week before.
Light rail uses one-way streets downtown; your outbound stop and return stop may differ.
Florin / South Sacramento riders can often skip the Elk Grove bus leg and board Blue Line closer to home.
transit.agencyNote: 2025 packet: “RT – Free Ride — SacRT provides free rides by scanning the coupon.” Assuming 2026 works the same; confirm when the 2026 packet drops. That coupon is the agency path — not a separate krewe ask to SacRT.
